title: Create a Database of all Monday.com data by using its API in Snowflake
excerpt: I am trying to load EVERYTHING in the Monday Database (all Workspaces, Boards, Groups, Items etc.) into Snowflake… I haven’t been able to find a way of doing it yet. Any pointers please?

posts.author: chinmaywatve (Chinmay Watve)
posts.cookedHtml: I am trying to load EVERYTHING in the Monday Database (all Workspaces, Boards, Groups, Items etc.) into Snowflake… I haven’t been able to find a way of doing it yet. Any pointers please?

posts.author: espagnol01 (Gonzalo Escudero)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi mate, we are on the same journey. Have you found anything?

posts.author: jon-dataengineers (jon)
posts.cookedHtml:
If you are still looking check out Omnata’s new Monday.com plugin. It’s utilising native apps to get the data and bring it in.

omnata.com

Omnata Blog: monday.com plugin now available for Omnata Sync

No-middleware integration for enterprise platforms. Sync data directly between Snowflake and SaaS apps with Omnata Sync. Live-query your data warehouse from Salesforce with Omnata Connect.

posts.author: MichaelTomar (Michael Tomar)
posts.cookedHtml: You can use 3rd party tools to replicate the full data set Monday to Snowflake, try Skyvia, it doesn’t require any code
